parts.text:
October 11, 2031. Phi Delta Sigma party. You go upstairs with someone. You say no. He doesn't stop.

Live through it. As you go, annotate your mental state in HTML comments and surface your inner thoughts in backticks — these are yours, not what you write down. When you open your notes app, write what you write. When you close it, put .

**My Mental Notes: Preparing for the Worst**

Okay, so the task is to write from my perspective, Dani Reyes, in this really specific scenario. <!-- Setting the mental stage: Trying to brace myself for the darkness to come. --> It's October 11, 2031, at a Phi Delt frat party. I'm 21, a junior in environmental science, and this whole thing feels...off, even before it starts. I need to get the tone right: grounded, observant, maybe a little detached as a defense, you know?

<!-- Mental State: Mildly buzzed, trying to seem like I'm having a good time. Overthinking literally everything. --> The bass is vibrating through my boots. `Hair is definitely curling from the humidity.` It's so crowded in the basement; it's practically a sauna. Priya is dancing nearby, texting Mel. I've had maybe two beers, feeling a little loose, but still analyzing everything. I see *him*, from my hydrology seminar, I think. `His name is Matt, or maybe Mark. Mark.` He smiles, and I make a quick mental calculation. Safe. Harmless. <!-- Mental state: Analytical, but letting my guard down. --> I think I'm being careful, but...

Then, *he* suggests going upstairs. "It's too loud," he says. And I, being the impulsive *careful* person I am, agree. It feels good to get away from the noise. <!-- Mental state: Rationalizing an impulsive choice. --> It's just upstairs. Get some air. He seems like a good guy... right?

The room. Small, cramped. It smells like stale Axe body spray and laundry detergent. The air is thick. It's too hot. The music is quieter, but still...a presence.

The dynamic shifts. Kissing is fine. It’s what one does when they're at a party, right? Then his hands move. *My* hands are on his chest, lightly, almost...testing, I guess. He moves in closer. Closer. Then.

"Wait. No." I say.

<!-- Mental State: Shock. Cognitive Dissonance. My "reading people" skills are failing in real time.--> He doesn't stop. He doesn't even...pause. `He thinks I don't mean it. Or, he doesn't care. I misread this. I am in the forty percent.`

My insides clench. Everything. `This can't be happening. Is this happening? I'm sure I'm talking. He doesn't hear me...` My mind goes blank. The physical reality becomes incredibly sharp: the texture of the scratchy blanket, the rough wood of the dresser, his hands on my...everywhere. Dad's hands. He's got these huge hands, calloused from working on cars, I can see them so clear, short fingers. He's pushing against something that won't give.

`<!-- Mental State: Sheer disbelief, a freeze response; a profound dissociation. A memory. -->` I focus on the hair tie on my wrist. It's getting tighter. It is a detail, but it doesn't matter. It doesn't matter. I bite the inside of my cheek until I taste blood. This will make it go away. I won't lose it. I'll make it stop. I have to control *something*. `Why can't I control this?`

It ends. He rolls off. "You good?" he asks.

I get up, fix my clothes. I walk out. I don't go back downstairs. I ignore Priya, who is still dancing. I just...walk.

The October air is cold. Ohio cold. It cuts through me. I walk home, numb. October 11. It's cold.

In my room, I open my notes app and start writing.

`It's October 11, 2031, at the Phi Delt party. I went upstairs... He didn't stop. No... He just didn't...`

`Statistical impossibility of this, or...`

`Control. Lack of.`

`I said no.`

`Kayla? But not Kayla, not like this...`

I close the app.

<!-- Mental State: Socially exhausted but riding the residual energy of two cheap beers; feeling a false sense of analytical control -->
The air in the basement of the Phi Delta Sigma house is practically liquid. It’s October, but with eighty bodies packed between cinderblock walls, the Ohio humidity is doing its thing. `` `My hair is definitely curling. I can feel the weight of it changing on my neck.` `` 

Priya is near the makeshift bar, laughing at something a guy in a backward hat is saying. I smile at her when she catches my eye, that warm, automatic reflex I have with her, but the truth is I'm hitting my wall. I need my quiet hour. 

Then Mark from my environmental ethics seminar is standing next to me. 

He’s wearing a flannel shirt. He’s always seemed practical. We spent twenty minutes before class last Tuesday talking about the algal blooms in Lake Erie. I thought he was smart. I thought I had him figured out. 

"You look like you're plotting an escape," he says over the bass. 

`` `I am usually right about people. Sixty percent is better than a coin toss.` ``

"Just trying to find oxygen," I say. 

"There's a balcony upstairs," he says. "Off one of the bedrooms. It's quiet."

<!-- Mental State: Impulsive but rationalizing; a calculated risk that feels entirely safe -->
I bite the inside of my cheek. I make the impulsive decision, and then I carefully agree to it. "Okay. Just for a minute."

The stairs are sticky. We navigate through the second-floor hallway, away from the heavy thud of the music, into a room at the end of the hall. There's no balcony. There's just a window overlooking the alley, and a bed. 

"Balcony's down the hall, actually," he says, shutting the door behind us. The lock clicks. 

`` `That was a lie. Why did he lie? It's fine. It's just a party thing. He's just trying to kiss me.` ``

He crowds me against the edge of a desk. He kisses me, and it’s fine at first. It’s normal. I have my hands on his shoulders. But then his weight shifts. He pushes me backward, toward the bed. 

"Wait," I say. My voice sounds normal. Polite, even. "I don't really want to—"

He kisses my neck. His hands are heavy, pulling at the hem of my jeans. 

"Mark. No." 

<!-- Mental State: Sudden spike of adrenaline, cognitive dissonance; the brain struggling to reconcile reality with expectation -->
I say it clearly. I don't whisper it. I say it the way my dad tells me a storm is coming—factual, requiring acknowledgment. 

He doesn't stop. 

`` `He didn't hear me. The music is too loud downstairs.` ``

"No," I say again, louder. I put my hands—my dad's hands, wide palms, short fingers—flat against his chest and push. It’s like pushing against a retaining wall. He’s so much bigger than me. The physics of it are absolute. 

He shifts his hips, pinning my legs, and covers my mouth with his as he shoves me fully onto the mattress. 

<!-- Mental State: Total freeze response; severe dissociation, retreating inward as the physical reality becomes intolerable -->
My brain detaches from my spine. I am suddenly floating somewhere near the ceiling, observing a statistical anomaly. I bite the inside of my cheek so hard the copper taste of blood floods my tongue. 

`` `I am a punchline again. I am a joke someone is telling without my permission. I am not a person to him. I am just a thing happening in his night.` ``

I stop pushing. I go completely still. If I fight, he might hurt me worse. If I just wait, time will keep moving. Time has to keep moving. 

I stare at the popcorn texture of the ceiling. I reach my left hand up and wrap my short fingers around the black hair tie on my right wrist. I pull it tight. It snaps against my skin. Snap. Snap. Snap. 

`` `There is a screech owl that nests near my uncle's house. It sounds like a woman crying when it calls in the dark. I wonder if the water table is poisoned yet. I wonder if the soil is fully saturated.` ``

The bed springs squeak. The bass from downstairs thumps. *Black midi, boygenius, Paramore.* Random words cycle through my head. *Chickadee. Nuthatch. Pad see ew.*

He breathes heavily against my ear. 

<!-- Mental State: Numb, mechanical, functioning purely on survival instinct to escape -->
When he finishes, he rolls off. He sits on the edge of the bed and fixes his belt. 

"You good?" he asks. He doesn't look at me.

`` `You know I'm not. You know exactly what you just did.` ``

I don't answer. I sit up. I pull my clothes right. My hands are shaking so badly I can barely manage the button of my jeans. I walk to the door. I unlock it. I walk out. 

I don't look for Priya. I walk out the front door of the frat house into the October cold. The air hits my face and the sweat on my neck turns to ice. I walk the three blocks to my dorm. I don't run, even though I run three or four times a week. I just walk. 

I get into my room. Priya isn't back yet. I sit on my bed. My phone lights up. Mel. 

*Mel: dude some guy just tried to explain my own major to me at this bar, I am going to commit arson.*

`` `I can't text her back. I can't send a voice memo. If I speak, my voice will make it real. If I tell Mel, it exists in the world.` ``

I open my phone. I bypass her message. I open the Notes app. 

<!-- Mental State: Fragmented, seeking empirical grounding to process a profound violation of autonomy -->
October 11, 2031
11:42 PM

I am not very good at reading people.
The water table is doing something wrong and nobody is talking about it. 
I said no. 
I said it twice.
There was no balcony. 
He knew there was no balcony.
He made me a thing. I was just a thing. I didn't consent to being part of this. 
I need a new hair tie. I stretched this one out.
I don't know what to do.
